top_signals.title: UAE Oil Exports Surge to 85% of Pre-War Levels, IEA Says - Bloomberg.com
top_signals.summary: UAE Oil Exports Surge to 85% of Pre-War Levels, IEA Says  Bloomberg.com
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Fans in China put politics aside to cheer Japan at World Cup
top_signals.summary: Chinese supporters of the Samurai Blue say their fandom has nothing to do with politics, reflecting instead a long admiration for Japanese soccer and what it represents for Asia.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Asia tech stocks mixed as Samsung rebounds, but chip shares remain under pressure after global selloff
top_signals.summary: Asia's technology stocks pared an earlier rebound to trade mixed on Wednesday after global equities posted steep losses in the previous session.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Renault reminds Nissan of its clout with ouster of ex-banker
top_signals.summary: The French carmaker, which holds 15% of voting rights, abstained from voting for the reappointment of influential outside director Motoo Nagai.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services, April 2026
top_signals.summary: The U.S. monthly international trade deficit decreased in April 2026 according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis and the U.S. Census Bureau. The deficit decreased from $56.6 billion in March (revised) to $55.9 billion in April, as exports increased more than imports. The goods deficit decreased $2.4 billion in April to $83.7 billion. The services surplus decreased $1.7 billion in April to $27.8 billion. Full Text

top_signals.title: Wizards pick AJ Dybantsa No. 1 overall in NBA draft
top_signals.summary: The Wizards tabbed Dybantsa ⁠over Kansas guard Darryn Peterson, who had a checkered college season at Kansas. The Utah Jazz then selected Peterson second overall.
